mechanism_narrative: A persistent positive month-over-month trend in industrial production reflects expanding real output and corporate earnings capacity, which supports S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon as investors price in continued profit growth.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between INDPRO 3-month trend and 60-day forward SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or positive INDPRO trend periods show no statistically significant excess return versus baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate crosses below a low threshold consistent with active accommodation, cheaper financing conditions boost equity valuations via lower discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, 20-day forward SPX returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or hit rate falls below 50% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deterioration in payroll employment growth and a rising unemployment rate signals a labor market inflection that precedes broad demand weakness, jointly driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month; neither series alone captures the regime shift.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change turns negative while UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over same period
regime_hint: labor market turning point / early contraction
failure_condition: Joint trigger (declining PAYEMS and rising UNRATE) fails to produce negative median 20-day forward returns, or occurs but SPX return distribution is statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline (p > 0.05) over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, a sustained decline in housing starts signals softening interest-rate-sensitive demand and forward-looking construction activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon as the market anticipates broader economic slowdown.
failure_condition: During late-cycle periods (identified by prior GDP growth deceleration), declining HOUST fails to precede negative 60-day forward SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A short-term acceleration in headline CPI above recent trend raises inflation expectations and risk of policy tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week as markets reprice rate expectations.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following CPI surprises above 0.4% MoM show no significant negative bias (p > 0.05) across at least 20 triggered instances in the sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in real retail sales indicates resilient consumer spending, the largest component of GDP, which supports corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS 3-month trend and 20-day forward SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP combined with contracting retail sales confirms a broad-based demand contraction that is not yet fully priced into equities, jointly producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter beyond what either series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ growth turns negative while RSAFS 3-month change is also negative
failure_condition: Joint negative GDPC1 and RSAFS signal fails to precede negative median 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of historical instances, or the interaction effect is not significantly stronger than either single-series signal (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above a high threshold indicating labor market distress, risk-averse repricing of equities dominates in the very short term, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week as markets react to confirmation of economic weakness.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following UNRATE crossing above 6.5% are not significantly negative (p > 0.05) across at least 15 triggered instances, or hit rate of negative returns falls below 50%.
